summary: Phase 1 foundation testing complete. 33/33 backend pytest tests pass (health, docs, OTP send/verify/resend limit, register happy + guards, login, /me, refresh rotation, logout, forgot-password, profile GET/PUT, membership validate-referral, admin login/stats/users/profile, cross-role guards). All frontend flows verified end-to-end via Playwright on public URL: landing hero + CTAs, PWA manifest.json (200) + sw.js (200), full 4-step registration with mobile 8649123012 -> RW090479, sponsor 'RIYORA Wellness' validated, dashboard welcome + membership ID + copy button + profile link + logout, profile update persistence (Karnataka->Maharashtra, Bengaluru->Mumbai), user login with password, forgot-password with new-password login, admin login with 9999999999/Admin@12345 -> stats cards (Total=5, Active=5, Memberships=5, OTPs=16) + users table.
critical_code_review_comments: seed_admin() (app/db/mongo.py) is not idempotent for password change: if an admin doc already exists, ADMIN_PASSWORD env changes are ignored on restart. Consider upserting the password_hash when it differs so ops can rotate the admin password via env.

utils/otp.py consume_verified_otp() has a dead if-branch — 'if expires_at < now + 10min: pass' does nothing (no-op). Either remove the block or actually enforce/allow the grace window. Currently harmless but confusing.
auth.py /logout swallows all decode errors silently via bare `except Exception: pass` — consider logging so refresh-token replay attempts are visible in audit.

CORS_ORIGINS is set to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers reject credentialed requests when origin is '*'. Cookies are not used today (JWT in localStorage) so functionally OK, but should be tightened to explicit origins before enabling cookie-based auth.

JWT tokens are stored in localStorage (Dashboard/Profile pages via api.js). XSS-exposed. If a future phase adds arbitrary user HTML/markdown, consider migrating to httpOnly cookies with the CORS fix above.
generate_membership_id retries up to 20 times against a 1M keyspace — safe today but log a warning when collision rate rises; will need a monotonic counter before ~100k users.

Register.jsx does not enforce password minimum length (8) client-side before allowing 'Continue' beyond the 'Passwords do not match' check when both are short; backend catches it via 422, but toast surfaces raw pydantic message. Minor UX polish.
test_credentials: Admin: 9999999999 / Admin@12345 (seeded). Dev OTP: 123456. Company referral: RW000000.
seed_data_creation: Test users are created inline via API and have full_name prefixed 'TEST_' or 'TEST UI' for easy identification. No teardown implemented — test mobiles are randomised so no dupes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite is at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — reuse it after future changes. It hits the public URL from frontend/.env. Frontend uses data-testid consistently; TID map is at /app/frontend/src/constants/testIds.js. PWA manifest.json + sw.js are reachable at the root. Programs/payments/activity/reports are intentionally out of scope for Phase 1 — do not test them.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues found. Only code-review polish items above.
